risks.description: The material line is partially legible as MS - 12.20, optional MS - 12.10. Routing treats it as a machinable cast/mild steel family pending confirmation.
risks.mitigation: Confirm final material grade and foundry material certificate before releasing tooling or machining programs.

risks.description: Datum B and datum C are interpreted as the right and left Ø9.00 locating hole axes; the exact C datum symbol and some control frames are not fully legible.
risks.mitigation: Confirm datum feature definitions and inspection method before hard-gauge or CMM programming.

risks.description: The Ø28.94 I/P socket-clearance features and Ø10.25/9.85 right-hole counterbore/clearance are partially ambiguous in the raster extraction.
risks.mitigation: Use the extracted defaults for provisional routing; verify on drawing/source CAD before NC release.

risks.description: Exact as-cast stock allowance and cored starting diameters for the large bores are not specified, creating clean-up and formula-input uncertainty.
risks.mitigation: Require foundry/pattern stock map and first-article machining stock verification, especially around Ø31 bores and datum A.

risks.description: The shop profile indicates no in-house CMM, while the drawing includes profile and position controls relative to A/B/C.
risks.mitigation: Use in-house gauges for production checks and outside CMM/FAI for first article or customer-required capability studies.

outside_processes_needed.reason: The drawing explicitly describes a cast component with draft, flash trimming, minimum wall thickness, bosses/ribs, and profile-controlled as-cast surfaces; the supplied shop profile does not list in-house foundry capability.
Casting route key rule requires stress relief/normalizing before machining to reduce distortion risk, unless the foundry supplies a certified stable/normalized casting condition.
outside_processes_needed.sequence_position: After casting clean-up and before datum machining

outside_processes_needed.reason: GD&T profile/position checks to datums A/B/C may require CMM because the shop profile marks CMM as not in-house.
outside_processes_needed.sequence_position: After deburr, cleaning, marking, and in-house dimensional inspection

feasibility.assessment_notes: Feasibility was pre-confirmed. The part envelope 234.88 × 149.94 × 55.0 mm fits the VMC 600 × 400 × 400 mm envelope when fixtured. Ø31.10/30.90 bores and Ø9 datum holes are within VMC/SPM boring, drilling, and reaming capability; outside CMM/FAI is recommended for GD&T verification.

stock.material: MS - 12.20, OPT: MS - 12.10; treated provisionally as machinable cast/mild steel pending material confirmation

starting_dimensions.notes: Near-net casting envelope taken from the finished drawing envelope. Pattern/foundry tooling must add local machining allowance on datum A, Ø31 bores, Ø9 holes, and F010; exact as-cast oversize is not specified in the feature graph.

stock.why: A near-net casting is selected because the drawing notes specify casting requirements, draft, flash trimming, minimum wall thickness, ribs/webs, bosses, and profile-controlled as-cast surfaces. At 1000 pieces, casting avoids excessive waste and machining time versus hogging the irregular body from block stock. Saw-cut stock is skipped per the casting/milling route rules; machining begins by establishing datum A on the casting.

evidence.cited_text: Casting minimum wall thickness 2.5 mm; 1° draft permissible except on mounting surface; trim or grind flash smooth; free from detrimental casting defects.
Shop profile lists CNC/VMC/SPM machining but no foundry; outside_vendor is available for outside_process_candidate and heat_treat.

route.route_name: Near-net cast component with VMC/SPM datum machining, precision boring, deburr, and inspection
route.route_reason: Composed from casting, milling, and hole-making route families. The casting sequence supplies the near-net body and requires stress relief before machining. Milling sequence drives datum A face machining first. Hole-making sequence drives spot-drill/drill/ream sequencing for datum holes and boring/chamfering sequence for the Ø31 bores. No turning route is used because the part is an irregular casting and cannot be economically or accurately spun as a lathe workpiece.

setups.setup_name: Outside foundry casting supply and stress relief
setups.machine_reason: The drawing defines a cast body, but the provided shop profile has no in-house foundry. Outside vendor is also the correct family for casting stress-relief/normalizing if not supplied certified by the foundry.

workholding.method: Foundry tooling, pattern, mold, and heat-treatment racks
workholding.grip_description: Near-net casting produced in foundry tooling; no machining workholding yet. Casting is handled by gates/risers/racks until supplied as a trimmed blank.
workholding.fixture_notes: Foundry pattern/core tooling must include shrinkage, draft, machining stock, and robust support for the long web and boss regions.

datum_references: Casting tooling datums only; machining datums A/B/C not yet established
setups.stock_state_before: Raw foundry charge material for MS - 12.20 / MS - 12.10 equivalent casting
setups.stock_state_after: Trimmed, cleaned, stress-relieved or normalized near-net casting with machining stock on datum A and machined holes/bores

sub_operations.operation_name: Procure/produce near-net cast body with bosses, web, pads, draft faces and cast radii
sub_operations.reason: These features are described as integral cast geometry and are controlled primarily by the casting profile/draft/min-wall requirements rather than by machining from solid.
sub_operations.notes: Foundry supply should include visual casting defect control, flash trimming/grinding, R1 unless otherwise specified, R25/R10 blends, and minimum 2.5 mm wall compliance.

sub_operations.operation_name: Stress-relieve or normalize cast blank before machining
sub_operations.reason: The casting route key rule requires stress relief before machining to reduce distortion as the datum face and bores are cut.
sub_operations.notes: If the foundry certifies the casting as normalized/stress-relieved and dimensionally stable, this may be satisfied by the supplied casting condition rather than a separate shipment.

setups.outside_reason: No foundry or heat-treatment furnace is listed in the Almity shop profile; outside_vendor is available for outside process candidates and heat treatment.
setups.inspection_at_setup: Review foundry certificate, heat-treatment/stress-relief certificate if applicable, and visually check for detrimental casting defects, flash, cold shuts, laps, and minimum stock on machined areas.
setups.setup_reason: Start with near-net cast geometry before any machining; casting and stress relief must precede datum machining.
assumptions: Casting is purchased/supplied near-net with enough local stock for datum A and bores.
Foundry stress relief or normalizing is required unless certified in supplied material condition.

setups.setup_name: Machine primary datum A bottom mounting face
setups.machine_reason: A 3-axis VMC is appropriate for the long flat datum face because the part fits the 600 × 400 × 400 mm envelope and the 0.25 mm flatness requirement is readily achievable by face milling. A lathe is not appropriate because the workpiece is an irregular casting, not a rotational blank.

workholding.method: Custom casting nest / adjustable 3-2-1 fixture on VMC table
workholding.grip_description: Hold on as-cast upper bosses/web and local pads with adjustable supports; orient datum A upward/exposed for face milling; clamp away from the full-length mounting face.
workholding.fixture_notes: Fixture must compensate for as-cast variation and support the 234.88 mm long body to avoid rocking or bending during face milling.

datum_references: As-cast external locating surfaces for first setup
F002 / datum A established by this setup
setups.stock_state_before: Stress-relieved near-net casting with as-cast bottom mounting surface and casting skin
setups.stock_state_after: Casting with machined datum A bottom face established to flatness 0.25

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough face mill datum A below casting skin
sub_operations.reason: Casting and milling key rules require datum faces first and first cut below cast skin before subsequent datum-referenced hole and bore operations.

tool.tool_description: Carbide face mill for cast steel/mild steel casting skin clean-up
tool.notes: First cut must get below abrasive casting skin.

sub_operations.notes: Use robust dust/chip control for casting scale and inclusions.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish face mill datum A to flatness 0.25
sub_operations.reason: Datum A must be a stable primary locating plane before the Ø9 datum holes and Ø31 bores are machined relative to A/B/C.

tool.tool_description: Finishing face mill or wiper-insert face mill
tool.notes: Climb-mill finish pass where fixturing allows.

sub_operations.notes: Do not machine as-cast top/draft faces unless source drawing confirms they are machined; general profile controls those surfaces.

sub_operations.operation_name: In-process inspect datum A flatness and clean-up
sub_operations.reason: Confirms the primary datum is usable before all datum-referenced hole and bore operations.

tool.tool_description: Surface plate, indicator/height gauge, and straightedge as appropriate for 0.25 flatness check

sub_operations.notes: If datum A does not fully clean up, reject or segregate casting before drilling datum holes.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Check flatness 0.25 and full clean-up of datum A.
setups.setup_reason: Datum A is the primary datum and must be machined before B/C locating holes and Bore R position-controlled machining.
assumptions: The bottom mounting face has sufficient cast stock for a first cut below casting skin and a finish clean-up pass.
Datum A is the only machined flat face explicitly required by the feature graph.

setups.setup_name: Machine Ø9 datum B/C locating holes and right counterbore
setups.machine_reason: For 1000 pieces, the repeated Ø9 datum holes and one coaxial counterbore are well suited to a dedicated SPM drilling/reaming fixture after datum A is established. The machine family supports drilling, reaming, tapping/chamfering-style hole operations and dedicated fixtures with careful tolerance capability.

workholding.method: Dedicated drill/ream fixture located on machined datum A
workholding.grip_description: Rest part on datum A, use fixture stops/nest for repeat orientation, clamp over robust cast pads while leaving the two lug holes and the right counterbore accessible from +Z.
workholding.fixture_notes: Fixture should include hardened locator pads and drill/ream guidance or controlled spindle locations; protect thin cast lugs from clamp distortion.

setups.stock_state_before: Casting with machined datum A; B/C holes still unmachined
setups.stock_state_after: Casting with datum B and C Ø9 holes reamed and right Ø10.05 counterbore/clearance machined

sub_operations.operation_name: Spot drill 2× Ø9 datum-hole positions
sub_operations.reason: Hole-making key rule: spot/center drill before twist drilling to prevent wander in datum holes.

tool.tool_description: Spot drill suitable for Ø9 datum holes
tool.notes: Spot depth set by NC program/tool geometry, not specified on drawing.

sub_operations.operation_name: Drill 2× Ø9 datum holes through 17.5 mm, leaving ream stock
sub_operations.reason: Drilling opens the through holes after spotting; reaming follows immediately in the same setup to preserve concentricity and positional accuracy.

tool.tool_description: Drill selected to leave appropriate stock for Ø9.00 reaming
tool.notes: Depth/diameter is below 3D, so peck drilling is not mandatory by route rule.

sub_operations.notes: Exact pre-ream drill diameter is a shop/CAM selection and is not specified in the drawing extraction.

sub_operations.operation_name: Ream 2× Ø9.00 datum B/C holes through 17.5 mm
sub_operations.reason: The Ø9 holes are datum/locating features with GD&T controls; reaming immediately after drilling in the same setup improves size, straightness, and axis quality.

tool.tool_description: Ø9.00 machine reamer for datum locating holes
tool.notes: Use pin gauge or bore gauge check after reaming.

sub_operations.notes: Datum C assignment is inferred and must be confirmed before gauge design.

sub_operations.operation_name: Machine right Ø10.05 × 9.0 deep counterbore/clearance coaxial with datum B hole
sub_operations.reason: The counterbore/clearance references the existing right Ø9 hole axis and is performed after the hole is established.

tool.tool_description: Counterbore/end mill suitable for Ø10.05 clearance feature
tool.notes: Feature F010 is low-confidence and must be verified before tool purchase.

sub_operations.notes: If F010 is actually a clearance diameter rather than a counterbore, update this operation to drill/ream/bore the confirmed feature.

sub_operations.operation_name: Inspect Ø9 datum holes and right counterbore/clearance
sub_operations.reason: B/C holes become the locating datums for the side-bore fixture; errors here propagate into Bore R and general profile inspection.

tool.tool_description: Ø9 pin gauges, depth gauge, bore gauge, and fixture check to datum A

sub_operations.notes: Confirm perpendicularity/position control interpretation before final inspection plan is frozen.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Pin-gauge Ø9 datum holes, check perpendicularity to datum A by fixture/indicator method, and inspect F010 depth/diameter if confirmed.
setups.setup_reason: Datum B and C holes must exist before the side-bore setup can locate from A/B/C.
assumptions: Datum B is the right Ø9 hole F008 and datum C is the left Ø9 hole F009.
F010 is a real coaxial counterbore/clearance feature on the right Ø9 hole.

setups.setup_name: Machine side-access Ø31 socket bores, Bore R, and Bore R chamfers
setups.machine_reason: The Ø31 bores require accurate coordinate positioning from datums A/B/C and interpolation/fine boring. A VMC with a custom angle fixture is more appropriate than a lathe because the part is an irregular casting and the bore axes are side-access features, not part-spindle axes.

workholding.method: Custom angle/tombstone fixture on VMC, located from datum A and pins in B/C holes
workholding.grip_description: Seat datum A on fixture pads, locate with hardened pins in F008/F009, rotate the part so the +Y/-Y bore axes are aligned with VMC spindle Z, and clamp on robust cast pads clear of bores and chamfer access.
workholding.fixture_notes: Fixture must preserve the A/B/C datum scheme and support the long web to avoid vibration during fine boring. Back-chamfer tool access is assumed for far-side Bore R chamfers.

setups.stock_state_before: Casting with datum A face, Ø9 B/C holes, and right counterbore complete; large side bores still as-cast/cored or undersize
setups.stock_state_after: Casting with Ø28.94 I/P clearance, 2× Ø31.00 sockets, Bore R Ø31.00, and Bore R 1.5 × 45° chamfers machined

sub_operations.operation_name: Bore 2× Ø28.94 I/P socket-clearance features
sub_operations.reason: F007 is coaxial with the two large sockets and is treated as the in-process/clearance geometry before final Ø31.00 boring.

tool.tool_description: Interpolation end mill or rough boring tool for Ø28.94 socket-clearance openings
tool.notes: Starting cored diameter is unknown; verify casting stock before final CAM.

sub_operations.notes: F007 is marked I/P and may not be a final deliverable feature; keep this operation provisional until confirmed.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough bore Bore R from cast/cored opening, leaving finish stock
sub_operations.reason: Casting route requires roughing all precision bore features before finishing; Bore R has a position control to A/B/C and should be opened before final boring.

tool.tool_description: Rough boring head or circular interpolation tool for Bore R
tool.notes: Exact cored/start diameter not available in feature graph.

sub_operations.notes: Bore start diameter is intentionally left null/unknown; confirm cored condition or add helical interpolation from solid if no core exists.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish bore 2× Ø31.00 sockets to 31.10/30.90 limits
sub_operations.reason: Finish boring establishes the two large socket bores after the Ø28.94 I/P clearance operation, using the A/B/C-located fixture.

tool.tool_description: Adjustable fine boring head or circular interpolation finishing tool for Ø31 sockets

sub_operations.notes: No honing/grinding is planned because the diameter tolerance is ±0.10 mm, not H7/H6 or tighter.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish bore Bore R Ø31.00 to 31.10/30.90 limits and position to A/B/C
sub_operations.reason: Bore R has the same diameter limits as the socket bores and a position control to A/B/C, so it must be finished after datums are established and after rough opening.

tool.tool_description: Fine boring head for Bore R
tool.notes: Use A/B/C-located fixture; verify position by gauge/CMM.

sub_operations.notes: Bore-start diameter is unknown from the drawing extraction and should be confirmed from casting/core design.

sub_operations.operation_name: Chamfer Bore R edges 4× 1.5 +0.10/-0.25 × 45°
sub_operations.reason: Bore R chamfers depend on the finished Bore R geometry and therefore follow finish boring.

tool.tool_description: 45° chamfer mill or front/back chamfer tool for Bore R edges
tool.notes: If back-chamfer tool is not available, add a separate -Y side fixture setup.

sub_operations.notes: The scope of the 4X chamfer callout should be confirmed; provisional default applies the chamfers to Bore R only.

sub_operations.operation_name: Inspect Ø31 bores, Bore R position, and Bore R chamfers
sub_operations.reason: Critical side bores and chamfers must be checked before deburr/cleaning because they define functional socket and datum-referenced geometry.

tool.tool_description: Bore gauge, plug gauges, chamfer gauge, and positional check fixture/CMM sample

sub_operations.notes: Use outside CMM for full GD&T confirmation if in-house positional measurement is insufficient.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Bore-gauge Ø31.10/30.90 limits, verify Bore R location relative to A/B/C, and gauge 1.5 × 45° chamfers.
setups.setup_reason: Large side bores are machined only after A/B/C datums are available; Bore R chamfers follow Bore R boring.
assumptions: Large bores are cast or cored undersize with enough stock for final boring.
A back-chamfer tool can reach far-side Bore R chamfers from this side setup; otherwise a supplemental -Y setup is required.

setups.setup_name: Finish bench deburr, clean, and mark
setups.machine_reason: Manual finish bench is the shop-profile machine family for deburr, edge break, cleaning, and simple marking. These operations do not require CNC capacity and should occur before final inspection.

workholding.grip_description: Support the casting on protected pads; manually access all machined holes, bore edges, cast edges, and external corners.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use protective trays to prevent damage to datum A and machined bores.

datum_references: C / F009 as needed for handling orientation
setups.stock_state_before: Fully machined casting with burrs and chips possible at holes, bores, datum face, and cast/machined edges
setups.stock_state_after: Deburred, cleaned, and marked component ready for final inspection/FAI

sub_operations.operation_name: Break all sharp edges 0.5 mm max and deburr holes/bores
sub_operations.reason: General note requires all sharp edges broken 0.5 max; hole-making route requires deburr after drilling/boring operations.

tool.tool_description: Hand deburr blades, abrasive points, countersink deburr tools, and soft abrasive pads
tool.notes: Do not over-break Bore R specified 1.5 × 45° chamfers.

sub_operations.notes: Pay attention to through-hole exits and bore edges; do not damage datum A flatness surface.

sub_operations.operation_name: Clean chips, casting dust, and abrasive residue
sub_operations.reason: Cleaning must occur after deburring and before marking/final inspection to prevent false measurements and contamination.

tool.tool_description: Air blow-off, brush, solvent/aqueous cleaning as allowed by material/process

sub_operations.notes: Ensure no abrasive or casting sand remains in bores.

sub_operations.operation_name: Mark part identification per drawing/shop requirement
sub_operations.reason: Marking is placed after cleaning and before final inspection per finish-bench-before-inspection rule.

tool.tool_description: Ink stamp, label, or low-stress marking method per customer requirement
tool.notes: Drawing-specific marking location is not provided in the feature graph.

sub_operations.notes: Do not mark on datum A or functional bore surfaces unless the drawing explicitly permits it.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Visual burr/edge-break check before release to inspection.
setups.setup_reason: Deburr, clean, and mark are final bench operations and must precede dimensional inspection.
assumptions: General 0.5 mm max edge break applies to all sharp edges not otherwise dimensioned.
No coating/plating is specified, so no surface-treatment setup is planned.

setups.machine_reason: The shop profile includes an in-house standard room for dimensional, visual, height-gauge, bore-gauge, and gauge-pin checks. This setup covers routine production inspection after all finish bench work.

workholding.method: Inspection fixture/surface plate located from A/B/C
workholding.grip_description: Seat datum A on inspection plate/fixture, use Ø9 datum pins for B/C where appropriate, and access bores and cast profiles with gauges or indicators.
workholding.fixture_notes: Dedicated production gauges are recommended for 1000-piece volume, especially for Ø9 datums, Ø31 bores, and Bore R position screening.

setups.stock_state_before: Deburred, cleaned, and marked machined component
setups.stock_state_after: In-house inspected component, ready for outside FAI if required or production release

sub_operations.operation_name: Final in-house dimensional and visual inspection of machined and cast features
sub_operations.reason: Final inspection verifies feature coverage before shipment or outside CMM/FAI: datum A flatness, Ø9 holes, Ø31 bores, counterbore, specified chamfers, casting profile, edge break, and visual casting condition.

tool.tool_description: Surface plate, height gauge, pin gauges, bore gauges, chamfer gauge, visual standards, and dedicated production gauges
tool.notes: Outside CMM recommended for full GD&T profile/position FAI.

sub_operations.notes: Routine in-house production checks can use gauges; full profile tolerance to A/B/C should be confirmed by CMM when required.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Full final in-house dimensional and visual inspection report for production release.
setups.setup_reason: Final inspection follows all machining, deburr, cleaning, and marking operations.
assumptions: Production inspection can be performed with in-house gauges except for CMM-only GD&T characteristics.
CMM/FAI is treated as outside first-article validation rather than routine 100% inspection.

setups.setup_name: Outside CMM / first article inspection for GD&T
setups.machine_reason: The shop profile lists CMM/FAI as an outside process candidate and indicates CMM is not in-house. Outside CMM is appropriate for profile of surface and position controls relative to datums A/B/C.

workholding.method: CMM fixture located from datum A and datum holes B/C
workholding.grip_description: Seat the component on datum A, constrain with Ø9 datum pins for B/C per drawing/MMC interpretation, and scan/check cast profile and Bore R position.
workholding.fixture_notes: CMM fixture should match the confirmed datum scheme and MMC datum modifiers.

setups.stock_state_before: In-house inspected component requiring first-article GD&T confirmation
setups.stock_state_after: Component with outside CMM/FAI report for GD&T profile/position acceptance

sub_operations.operation_name: CMM/FAI verify profile, datum scheme, Ø9 holes, Bore R position, and Ø31 bores
sub_operations.reason: The drawing contains ASME Y14.5-style profile and position controls to A/B/C, and the shop profile does not provide in-house CMM capability.

tool.tool_description: Outside CMM with datum A/B/C fixture and appropriate probe styli
tool.notes: Scope may be FAI/capability sample rather than every production piece.

sub_operations.notes: Use confirmed GD&T datum interpretation before final FAI programming.

setups.outside_reason: CMM/FAI is listed as outside_vendor capability because cmm_in_house is false in the shop profile.
setups.inspection_at_setup: Outside CMM/FAI report for profile of cast surfaces, Bore R position, datum A flatness correlation, and datum B/C hole relationships.
setups.setup_reason: CMM/FAI is the final validation step after all manufacturing and in-house checks.
assumptions: Outside CMM is required for first article unless customer accepts in-house gauge inspection only.

routing_notes: Casting is the chosen stock/process because the body contains draft, flash notes, minimum wall thickness, ribs, pads, bosses, and an irregular envelope; machining from block is not selected.
Saw cutting is intentionally skipped because the selected stock form is a near-net casting, consistent with the retrieved casting and milling route rules.
CNC turning is not used even though Ø31 bores exist; the part has no dominant spindle axis as a workpiece and the bores are machined in fixtures by VMC boring/interpolation.
Datum A is machined first, then datum B/C holes are drilled and reamed, then Bore R and other side bores are machined from A/B/C as required by the datum scheme.
The Ø28.94 I/P feature is treated as an in-process clearance/rough-bore state for the two socket bores; confirm whether it is final deliverable geometry.
If the four Bore R chamfers cannot be made with a front/back chamfer tool from the +Y side setup, add a supplemental -Y VMC setup for back-side chamfering.
No heat-treat-after-machining, grinding, honing, coating, plating, threading, welding, or assembly operations are planned because none are specified in the feature graph.
setup_time_min values are set to 0.0 placeholders; no setup-time or cycle-time estimates were calculated in this route output.

clarifications.question: Confirm the title-block material specification and revision/issue field. The material reads like 'MS - 12.20, OPT: MS - 12.10', and the revision field is not clearly legible.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Material grade controls foundry sourcing, heat-treatment/stress-relief expectations, cutting-tool selection, and material certification.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use material raw text 'MS - 12.20, OPT: MS - 12.10' and treat as machinable cast/mild steel until confirmed.

clarifications.question: Confirm datum feature definitions for B and C and the exact feature-control frames on the two Ø9.00 holes and Bore R.
clarifications.why_it_matters: B/C datum interpretation controls the drilling fixture, side-bore fixture, production gauges, and CMM/FAI program.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat datum A as F002, datum B as right Ø9 hole F008, and datum C as left Ø9 hole F009.

clarifications.question: Confirm whether the drawing contains two or three final Ø31.10/30.90 bores and whether the 4X 1.5 × 45° chamfer note applies only to Bore R or to additional bores.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Bore and chamfer count affects tooling, fixture access, cycle time, and feature coverage.
clarifications.suggested_default: Model two Ø31.10/30.90 bores in F005 plus one Bore R in F006; apply the 4X chamfer to Bore R only.

clarifications.question: Confirm actual through-depth/axial length for the Ø31.10/30.90 bores and Ø9.00 holes.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Depth is a required formula input for drilling, reaming, boring, and inspection operations.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use 15.0 mm axial length for large socket bores and 17.50 mm through thickness for the Ø9.00 holes.

clarifications.question: Clarify the meaning and final applicability of the '2X Ø28.94 I/P SOCKET CLEARANCE' callout and the adjacent 'Ø10.25/9.85' feature near the right Ø9 hole.
clarifications.why_it_matters: These may be in-process or clearance features rather than final controlled geometry; incorrect interpretation changes routing and inspection requirements.
clarifications.suggested_default: Keep Ø28.94 as an in-process socket-clearance feature and Ø10.25/9.85 as a coaxial counterbore/clearance until confirmed.

clarifications.question: Confirm the casting/core design: cored starting diameters for the Ø31 bores, local machining stock on datum A, and whether the foundry supplies a stress-relieved/normalized casting certificate.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Cored starting diameters and stock allowance are required for accurate boring cycle inputs and to avoid failure-to-clean-up in production.
clarifications.suggested_default: Assume near-net casting with sufficient local stock, Ø28.94 I/P rough clearance for the two sockets, and foundry stress-relief/normalizing before machining.

clarifications.question: Confirm whether customer requires outside CMM/FAI for every submission or only first article / PPAP sampling.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The shop profile has in-house standard-room inspection but no in-house CMM, so CMM scope affects routing, lead time, and outside processing.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use outside CMM/FAI for first article and rely on in-house production gauges for routine checks.

assumptions.text: The production context quantity of 1000 pieces is used for process selection even though the title block quantity is null.
MS - 12.20 / MS - 12.10 is treated as a machinable cast/mild steel material family for provisional routing.
The component is supplied as a one-piece near-net casting from an outside foundry because the shop profile does not include foundry capability and the drawing contains casting-specific notes.
Datum B is the axis of the right Ø9 hole F008 and datum C is the axis of the left Ø9 hole F009.
The 15.0 mm dimension is used as the axial length/depth for the Ø31 bores, and 17.5 mm is used as the through thickness for the Ø9 holes, per the Agent 1 extracted defaults.
F007 Ø28.94 I/P is treated as an in-process clearance/rough-bore state for the two F005 socket bores rather than a separate post-finish operation.
F010 is treated as a right-hole counterbore/clearance Ø10.05 × 9.0 deep even though the extraction confidence is low.
Routine production inspection can be performed in-house with gauges, while full GD&T CMM/FAI is outsourced as needed.
Setup-time fields are 0.0 placeholders because this route output does not calculate setup times or costs.

analysis_trace.summary: Reconstructed an irregular one-piece cast component, approximately 234.88 × 149.94 × 55.0 mm, with datum A as the bottom mounting face, two inferred Ø9 datum holes B/C, two large Ø31 socket bores, one Ø31 Bore R, cast ribs/webs/pads/draft faces, R25/R10 cast radii, Bore R chamfers, and general edge break.
issues: Datum C and several GD&T details are inferred.
Large-bore and Ø9-hole depths are interpreted from nearby dimensions.

analysis_trace.summary: Called the knowledge library for casting, milling, and hole_making. Casting applies because the drawing contains casting notes, draft, minimum wall thickness, ribs, bosses, and flash trimming. Milling applies for datum A face milling and fixture-based machining. Hole-making applies for spot/drill/ream Ø9 datum holes, counterbore, and precision boring of Ø31 features.
issues: Turning route was not selected because the whole part is not rotational and cannot be held as a lathe workpiece.
Grinding/honing/heat-treatment-after-machining were not selected because no H7/H6 bore, hardened condition, or post-HT precision requirement is specified.

analysis_trace.summary: Classified the part as hybrid and complex: casting is the stock-forming route, with VMC/SPM milling and hole-making for machined datums, holes, bores, and chamfers.
issues: The available classification set has no pure 'cast' family, so hybrid best represents casting plus machining.

analysis_trace.summary: Called organizational routing corrections with part_family=hybrid, base_geometry=complex, material_class=mild_steel, key features including casting, datum holes, precision bores, and volume 1000. No corrections were returned.

Selected a near-net casting rather than plate/block/bar because the geometry is irregular with cast bosses, webs, draft, minimum wall, and flash notes. At 1000 pieces, casting tooling is justified and minimizes material removal.
issues: Exact as-cast oversize and cored bore diameters are not defined in the feature graph.
Stock dimensions are therefore tied to the finished envelope with local machining allowance noted rather than exact foundry blank dimensions.

analysis_trace.summary: Composed the route as outside foundry casting and stress relief, VMC datum A face milling, SPM/VMC-style datum hole drilling/reaming and F010 counterbore, VMC side boring of F007/F005/F006 and Bore R chamfers, finish-bench deburr/clean/mark, in-house inspection, and outside CMM/FAI.
issues: Back-side Bore R chamfer access assumes a front/back chamfer tool; otherwise an added -Y VMC setup is needed.
Stress relief is included from casting-route best practice even though no explicit heat-treatment note is extracted.

analysis_trace.summary: Decomposed each setup into atomic operations: rough/finish face milling, spot/drill/ream/counterbore, rough and finish bore operations, chamfer milling, deburr, cleaning, marking, inspection, and CMM/FAI. Formula inputs are populated from feature graph dimensions where available; unknown cored start diameters are left null and clarified.
issues: Some formula inputs, such as rough Bore R start diameter and exact spot-drill depth, are not available from the drawing extraction.
Process dimensions like face-mill axial depth and pass count are planning assumptions rather than drawing dimensions.

analysis_trace.summary: Verified that all feature IDs F002 through F022 are covered by at least one sub-operation. Manufacturing order is logical: casting/stress relief → datum A → datum holes B/C → side bores/chamfers → deburr/clean/mark → inspection/FAI. Workholding keeps a stable datum progression using A then B/C pins for the side-bore fixture. Key rules are satisfied: datums first, center drill before drilling, ream in same setup, rough before finish for large bores, chamfers after bore, and inspection after finish bench.
issues: Full GD&T verification depends on confirmed datum interpretation and outside CMM availability.
Feature coverage includes as-cast features through the outside casting and final inspection steps rather than machining them individually.
